title: Streaming JSON, CSV, Accessible HTML, and Remediation Workflows · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deterministic accessible report suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Version record envelopes, ordering, missingness, error rows, provenance, encoding, newline, and digest manifests for streaming JSON.

options.text: Another successful Remediation Lineage run of the same deterministic accessible report suite happy path. That claim fails the remediation lineage · fault isolation evidence boundary for Remediation Lineage.

For remediation lineage · fault isolation, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Remediation Lineage for deterministic accessible report suite.
A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Remediation Lineage.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Remediation Lineage
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-remediation-lineage-d1czy3" that uses case 9283 to prove streaming JSON, CSV dialect, deterministic order, accessible HTML, and remediation lineage. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Remediation Lineage.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Remediation Lineage into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Remediation Lineage, keep Remediation Lineage connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
